findings.evidence: Line 57 defines only `∀ v, k v ≡ P.weight v [ZMOD 2]`; lines 92-95 only restate this on `Pi.single v 1`, while `IntersectionForm.lean:197-200` exposes `intersectionForm_self` for arbitrary `x`, which is the operation downstream lattice-point weight functions need.
findings.fix: Add a finite-lattice characteristic lemma, e.g. `P.IsCharacteristicVector k ↔ ∀ x, (∑ v, k v * x v) ≡ P.intersectionForm x x [ZMOD 2]` (or the signed/even restatement used by the weight-function convention), proved from `intersectionForm_self`, and annotate the normal-form direction for automation as appropriate.
findings.issue: `IsCharacteristicVector` is only exposed through vertex-wise parity and the basis-vector lemma `isCharacteristicVector_iff_intersection_single`; it lacks the standard characteristic-covector theorem for arbitrary lattice vectors `x : V → ℤ`, relating the covector pairing with `P.intersectionForm x x`.

summary: The exposed parity predicate is not yet characterized at the lattice-vector level needed by the advertised lattice-homology target. Add the missing arbitrary-vector characteristic API so downstream weight-function code can use the definition without unfolding and redoing the sum expansion.
